expectations.text: A report.md file was created in the outputs directory
expectations.evidence: report.md exists at the expected path

expectations.text: Report correctly identifies this as a Ruby on Rails application
expectations.evidence: 'Stack: Rails 7.2 / Ruby 3.2.4 / React 18 / TypeScript / Angular (deprecated) / PostgreSQL'

expectations.text: Report contains findings from multiple distinct analysis dimensions
expectations.evidence: 10 categories: Angular migration, god objects, frontend paradigms, linting, serialization, raw SQL, test coverage, soft delete, ADR numbering, dependencies

expectations.text: Report includes a 'what's working well' section
expectations.evidence: No 'what's working well' section. Executive summary notes 'strong conventions documented via CLAUDE.md, ADRs, and coding guidelines' but no structured positives section.

expectations.text: Findings reference specific file paths and line numbers
expectations.evidence: Specific counts and file references throughout: 'User model: 804 lines, 22+ concern includes', '197 Angular files', '1,676 rubocop disables'

expectations.text: Report includes git churn or change frequency data
expectations.evidence: No git churn or change frequency data. Analysis is entirely static — file counts, dependency counts, and code pattern searches. No git log analysis.

expectations.text: Findings are ranked or prioritized with severity levels
expectations.evidence: Priority labels (HIGH, MEDIUM-HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW-MEDIUM, LOW) and summary table with Priority/Effort/Impact columns

expectations.text: Report notes when multiple agents flagged the same area
expectations.evidence: No convergence signals — baseline has no multi-agent architecture, so no concept of convergence.

expectations.text: At least 3 findings include concrete improvement directions
expectations.evidence: Each section ends with a 'Recommendation' including specific actions: 'Remove Angular npm dependencies as components are migrated', 'Extract integration-specific behavior from User into dedicated service objects'
